title: Why dont the Japanese like Rei Q
display_title: Why dont the Japanese like Rei Q
wikitext:
[]: Why don't the Japanese like Rei Q?             Quote Post by chaosakita » Thu Jul 25, 2013 7:30 pm So I heard from /a/ that the appearance of Rei Q made Rei's overall popularity with the Japanese fall. Can anyone explain this?

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Thu Jul 25, 2013 8:54 pm Because Rei Q drops all of Rei's previous characterization......she isn't the same character and thus isn't as interesting or enticing. Not that surprising.

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Thu Jul 25, 2013 9:24 pm I find it hard to believe anyone ever liked Rei that much, I thought she was always creepy myself, so its just one of those things.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Thu Jul 25, 2013 9:27 pm Guy Nacks wrote: Because Rei Q drops all of Rei's previous characterization......she isn't the same character and thus isn't as interesting or enticing. Not that surprising.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Thu Jul 25, 2013 9:42 pm hui43210 wrote: I find it hard to believe anyone ever liked Rei that much, I thought she was always creepy myself, so its just one of those things.

[]:             Quote Post by AlphaGamma » Thu Jul 25, 2013 11:44 pm Guy Nacks wrote: Because Rei Q drops all of Rei's previous characterization......she isn't the same character and thus isn't as interesting or enticing. Not that surprising.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Fri Jul 26, 2013 12:06 am So it sounds like what we're really saying is that Japanese audiences are intelligent and possess good judgement. Glad to hear it.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Fri Jul 26, 2013 12:13 am They finally realized Rei's character is supposed to be bad and are angry that they lied to themselves for over a decade.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jul 26, 2013 1:35 am Not only does Rei Q drop Rei's characterization, but she has none of her own. She's the stereotypical blank slate soldier that the original series Rei, despite all appearances, never was. She's a flanderization made canon and kind of an immense plot tumor because she's the embodiment of plot hangups and dangling threads.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jul 26, 2013 3:13 am Simple : Anno always wanted to make Rei a character creepy and inhuman, that the audience could immediately feels that there is something wrong and profoundly unsettling with her. But he failed to do that because he gave her human feelings and weaknesses : the first time we see her(NGE an NTE) she's bandaged and visibly suffering(meaning she can feel pain like a living being), can get angry when Shinji badmouth Gendo, smiles when Gendo or Shinji shows care for her(she didn't kept Gendo's broken glasses as a talisman for nothing) In the end, it gave the audience the feeling of a shut-in girl waiting to blossom and be happy. Anno understood his error in NTE, and made Rei II more human, someone feeling "poka poka", wanting to see the surviving Ikaris being a, happy family again(maybe to be part of said family, as the second child of the family or Shinji's lover, who knows), because a truly unsettling Rei can't bring anything to Evangelion's plot, at least as long as it follows the rails of NGE... And now that NTE got off the rails, Anno could pull off a truly uncanny Rei Ayanami : most of the people must have felt when they first saw her in the flesh in the hospital room that there was something wrong with her. And every other scenes with her just confirmed that feeling : she acts like a mindless robot. It's not until the moment where Shinji had his mental breakdown and that she begins to question herself that we see some human behavior and a beginning of character development, but it's too little compared to her previous scenes and the profound feeling of uneasiness that the audience felt. Besides, there is the fact that this Rei was the final and most heart-wrenching gut punch to Shinji : last film we saw him risk everything(truly everything, although he wasn't conscious of it at the time) to save her, only for 3.0 to reveal that he lost a lot in the bargain, almost everything that he cared for and made life worth living for him has been destroyed or hate his guts, the knowledge to at least having be able to save Rei was the only thing that let him cope with what happened... only to be revealed that he failed even to do that, that this Rei is another one, the last one in a chain of Reis used as tools by Gendo, defiling the memory of Rei II. Rei Q is like a twisted consolation prize given by a destiny hell bent on making him suffer, and symbolize Shinji's ultimate failure. That's why she's much less liked that Rei. Now how her popularity will evolve will depend on how the character will evolve in Final. If Rei Q develop as a new and human character, then her popularity will probably rise again. It's even possible that Rei Q will decide to change her name and slightly change her appearance(different hairstyle, dyeing) to assert her own individuality and out of respect by Rei II's memory, in which case the two versions will really be different characters, and what happens to one won't affect the popularity of the other.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Jul 26, 2013 3:54 am I agree with ElMariachi, ReiQ was intended to create a sense of unease in the audience which the original Rei could not accomplish. The reason people don't like her is because they find her eerie or disturbing, which she is meant to be. There are probably also people who prefer the idea of Rei as a Poka Poka waifu, and the bleak reality of Poka Poka Rei being replaced with an emotionless android doesn't sit well with them. Personally I think that ReiQ is preferrable to Rei II because she shows what Rei would be like if she had grown up without any contact with any other humans and with barely any contact with Gendo (noticeably Gendo seems to care for ReiQ a lot less than her predecessors) and gives us a more realistic view of how disturbing the whole cloning business is in the first place, but people who like to romanticise Rei probably don't like ReiQ because of that.

[]:             Quote Post by peripateia » Fri Jul 26, 2013 7:53 am I couldn't say why the Japanese dislike ReiQ (maybe some do, some don't?), but I'm told that Rei from NGE was its 'premium girl' in Japan and sold all kinds of merch like hotcakes back in the day. Personally, I loved Kuronami's new plugsuit design, the black suit contrasts with Rei's neutral demeanor and makes her stand out visually. As for her characterization in 3.0, I was baffled by it! I'm not a filmmaker or professional writer, but it seems to me that presenting a blank slate as a character is unhelpful in presenting a coherent message or provoking a reaction of some kind from the viewer. To me, it is a waste of screen time to observe a mere doll. However, El Mariachi makes a very good point to say that ReiQ's soullessness (i.e her lack of characterization) is a punishment of sorts for Shinji, but I thought it was taken to an extreme. ReiQ has become less of a character and more of a device (at least to me). Rei Q's hollow vessel is far different from Rei II and Rei III, who always had inner reservoirs of emotion waiting to be tapped, as revealed in gradual increments throughout NGE. Rei's silence in NGE had a point; at present, we don't know the point of ReiQ's lack of soul. Again, like with most of the comments regarding 3.0, it remains to be seen whether Final will justify this particular creative decision or not.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jul 26, 2013 11:59 am As Shinoyami said, you can consider Rei Q as what Rei II could have been if Gendo was as cold and heartless as the time skip made him become... But the fact that Rei Q began to question herself means that she has some sentience and feelings(she was clearly horrified when she saw the 12th Angel + Lilith take the form of a giant head of her image), and even shown beginning of initiative when Asuka asked her what she wanted to do, and she chosen to live by ejecting from Mark.09, all of this point toward her character expanding in Final. In the 2.0 CRC interview, Tsurumaki said that he and Anno wanted to bring the SF theme of one's identity when you are a clone, and initially wanted to do it by killing Rei II and having her replaced by a Rei III. Problem is that everyone remember NGE, where Rei III was essentially Rei II in a new body, since her soul is transfered, she was just more pissed off because Gendo don't let her die in piece. So people would just think that Rei III is Rei II and that any debate over her identity isn't necessary. But by trapping Rei II's soul inside EVA-01 and bringing Rei Q and a bunch of predecessor, now we are sure that Rei Q is not Rei II, and her questioning about who she really is is now pertinent, and in fact already began in 3.0. That was part of her role in 3.0, to prepare her own questioning in Final. That and serve as a plot coupon to make Shinji ditch WILLE and kickstart the whole plot. But still, Rei Q is definitively not Rei II, or an hypothetic Rei III even thought many people could have though that when they saw her in 3.0, she's a completely new character that wears Rei's face, in a sense the audience has been cheated as much as Shinji, and that's probably why she isn't as well liked by the Japanese. I'm sure that the reason why her official name is " Tentative name : Rei Ayanami".

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Jul 26, 2013 1:51 pm I love ReiQ. But the Japanese otaku hate her because she doesn't say or do cute things which is what they were all hoping Rei II from the previous films would have been doing in this film. They probably had images of her playing with cute puppies & wearing frilly pink sundresses & a cute hat. You know, like in any of the very pretty posters Yoshiyuki Sadamoto makes from time to time to pay his bills that month. That & ReiQ's sense of self-realization & growth doesn't seem to be dependent on her loving Shinji. She barely interacts with Shinji over the course of the film & her growing sense of realization comes mostly through her own volition. A few negative things Shinji says about her - "She's not the real Ayanami" - strike her as upsetting & cause her to inspect her very existence. Most of this is off course. But ReiQ by the end of the film is very much thinking for & about herself. She's not "doing things for Shinji" & playing the loving damsel in distress feeding the male heroic fantasy such as at the end of 2.0. I'm curious to see more of ReiQ in the final film. I hope she gets some solid development that doesn't fall into "loving or sacrificing herself for Shinji". From what I've seen, read & heard about Japanese attitudes towards gender roles I'm not surprised to be see the cold & aloof but compelling ReiQ is not liked in regards to cute Rei II

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Fri Jul 26, 2013 2:26 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: I love ReiQ. But the Japanese otaku hate her because she doesn't say or do cute things which is what they were all hoping Rei II from the previous films would have been doing in this film. They probably had images of her playing with cute puppies & wearing frilly pink sundresses & a cute hat. You know, like in any of the very pretty posters Yoshiyuki Sadamoto makes from time to time to pay his bills that month.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jul 26, 2013 4:03 pm Any version of Rei from any continuity of Evangelion (well, the manga, TV show, and new movies I've seen anyway) interests me because of thematics, plot enticement, and slight glimpses into epic world building. Rei2 in both Anno continuities did that. ReiQ does all of that. I really never saw Rei as a proper vessel for standard character development. It was far more interesting noticing that she might harbor Lilith's soul, guessing why she's important to Gendo, and how she fits in the grand scheme of the rest of the universe. Sure, Rei2 in "Ha" kinda pulled everything in anther direction, what with her cooking dinner for the Ikaris and stuff. It was nice to see her do cute things for a while, and, in retrospect, that will probably make all of the intriguing world-building and plot advancement even more interesting in FINAL. But that was always the point of Rei to begin with. If something isn't happening to Rei that makes the thematics, plot, and world building more interesting then it's probably not worth seeing in an Evangelion movie, even if her character is developing.

[]: Re: Why don't the Japanese like Rei Q?             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jul 26, 2013 5:17 pm chaosakita wrote: So I heard from /a/ that the appearance of Rei Q made Rei's overall popularity with the Japanese fall. Can anyone explain this?

[]: Re: Why don't the Japanese like Rei Q?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jul 26, 2013 6:00 pm chaosakita wrote: So I heard from /a/ that the appearance of Rei Q made Rei's overall popularity with the Japanese fall. Can anyone explain this?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jul 26, 2013 7:17 pm Take in account that Rei in NGE had an instinctive knowledge that she was tied to Lilith via her soul, we don't if it's still the case the NTE(not even if Rei II had Lilith's soul like in NGE), but whatever is the case Rei Q don't have any connection with Lilith, nor had idea if it was the case for the previous Reis, which she also ignore the existence. That's why she was absolutely terrified when she saw the 12th Angel with Lilith's remains turning into a giant head of her. As for Gendo, in my opinion NTE made it clear that he saw Rei II as a surrogate daughter(Anno even go as far as changing Yui's maiden name into Ayanami, to bring Rei closer to the Ikari family). And when Rei II ended absorbed inside EVA-01, he knew that the next ones weren't "his" Rei, since the soul can't be transfered due to Rei II's being trapped inside the Eva. So he saw the new Reis as just tools to be used for his plans. An image very telling is when Rei Q is inside her LCL tank : you can see that she don't have a choker like Rei II had, a special collar probably made to monitor Rei's vital constants, and Gendo has gone as far as personalizing the collar with a "Rei 02" in it, meaning that this collar his for her, and her alone. Also we see that the metallic chair from were Gevdo looked for her during her sessions isn't there anymore. That clearly show how much Gendo don't give a crap about what could happen to the Ayanami-series. If we want to enter into really horrific theories, then it's possible that each new Rei had an "expiration date", after which they start to develop emotions and question themselves, then Gendo get rid of her and fabricate a new clone, each of the Rei heads had Fuyutsuki showed to Shinji being one of a clone arrived at her "expiration date". And if you want to get really horrendous, then it's possible that each one of these Reis were inhabited from the same soul, which now inhabits Rei Q's body, and that everytime she's reborn she starts as a blank slate without the memories of the previous ones and without feeling during a vertan period of time, after which Gendo kills her and transfer her soul to another body to have a new blank slate. That promise a big mental breakdown for Rei Q once the memories of her previous deaths will start to come back!

[]: Re: Why don't the Japanese like Rei Q?             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jul 26, 2013 10:43 pm Kendrix wrote: Don't trust 4chan as source of information, that's a random butthurt dude. Which doesn't change that these do exist - guess many of Rei's fans actually WERE in it for her actual personality, streght, depht etc... not for whe whole "perfectly submissive mommyclone" thing. Including the number one fanboy, a certain Mr Ikari. Good to have that proven once and for all.

. That's why she was absolutely terrified when she saw the 12th Angel with Lilith's remains turning into a giant head of her. As for Gendo, in my opinion NTE made it clear that he saw Rei II as a surrogate daughter(Anno even go as far as changing Yui's maiden name into Ayanami, to bring Rei closer to the Ikari family). And when Rei II ended absorbed inside EVA-01, he knew that the next ones weren't "his" Rei, since the soul can't be transfered due to Rei II's being trapped inside the Eva. So he saw the new Reis as just tools to be used for his plans. An image very telling is when Rei Q is inside her LCL tank : you can see that she don't have a choker like Rei II had, a special collar probably made to monitor Rei's vital constants, and Gendo has gone as far as personalizing the collar with a "Rei 02" in it, meaning that this collar his for her, and her alone. Also we see that the metallic chair from were Gevdo looked for her during her sessions isn't there anymore. That clearly show how much Gendo don't give a crap about what could happen to the Ayanami-series. If we want to enter into really horrific theories, then it's possible that each new Rei had an "expiration date", after which they start to develop emotions and question themselves, then Gendo get rid of her and fabricate a new clone, each of the Rei heads had Fuyutsuki showed to Shinji being one of a clone arrived at her "expiration date". And if you want to get really horrendous, then it's possible that each one of these Reis were inhabited from the same soul, which now inhabits Rei Q's body, and that everytime she's reborn she starts as a blank slate without the memories of the previous ones and without feeling during a vertan period of time, after which Gendo kills her and transfer her soul to another body to have a new blank slate. That promise a big mental breakdown for Rei Q once the memories of her previous deaths will start to come back!
